name: Neighborhood Trust Federal Credit Union
description_short: Community development credit union serving Upper Manhattan since 1997, offering low-cost financial services and affordable loans to underserved communities.
description_long:
Neighborhood Trust Federal Credit Union (NTFCU) was founded in 1997 with a mission to serve the underserved communities of Upper Manhattan in New York. The organization is structured as a non-profit financial cooperative regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and certified as a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) by the U.S. Department of the Treasury. NTFCU was the first credit union in the United States to receive Juntos Avanzamos accreditation in 2017, reflecting its deep commitment to the Latino community and financial inclusion.

NTFCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services including savings and checking accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loans, small business loans, credit cards, home loans, auto loans, and free tax preparation. The credit union provides online and mobile banking through their digital platform and accepts ITIN numbers for personal and business loans, making financial services accessible to individuals regardless of immigration status. Members can also access Zelle for money transfers and add their credit cards to digital wallets.

What distinguishes NTFCU is its explicit commitment to financial inclusion and community development. As a democratically-owned cooperative, all members have shareholder rights and can be elected to the Board of Directors. The institution provides financial education programs, scholarship opportunities, and a financial wellness program alongside competitive loan rates. The leadership team includes executives with deep community ties, and the board comprises community leaders and financial professionals dedicated to the credit union's mission.

As a smaller, community-focused credit union rather than a large bank, NTFCU may have more limited branch locations and services compared to major financial institutions. However, for individuals specifically seeking to work with a mission-driven organization that prioritizes underserved communities and provides bilingual services in Upper Manhattan, this represents a meaningful alternative to traditional banking.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Accepts ITIN numbers for personal and business loans, serving immigrant populations
Non-profit structure with democratically-owned governance—members are shareholders
CDFI certified by U.S. Department of Treasury, first U.S. credit union with Juntos Avanzamos accreditation
Offers free tax preparation and financial wellness programs to members
Provides financial education and scholarship opportunities alongside banking services
Federal deposit insurance up to $250,000 backed by U.S. Government
Digital banking and mobile app available; credit cards can be added to digital wallets
cons: Limited to Upper Manhattan service area; may not serve customers outside New York
As a smaller credit union, fewer physical branches and potentially less robust customer service infrastructure than major banks
Website lacks specific interest rate information, making rate comparison difficult for potential members
best_for: Immigrants and non-citizens seeking credit access without traditional documentation
Upper Manhattan residents seeking community-oriented banking with mission-driven values
Individuals prioritizing financial education and building credit through a non-profit institution
Small business owners seeking affordable lending with community development focus
diagnosis: NTFCU is best for underserved populations in Upper Manhattan—particularly immigrants, Latino community members, and those building credit—who value mission-driven banking over conventional institutional services. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to the Upper Manhattan area and the smaller institution's capacity compared to major banks.
services: Credit cards with digital wallet integration
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1112 St Nicholas Ave, New York, NY 10032
